free_text: Resonance neutron-capture cross sections of stable
magnesium isotopes and their astrophysical
implications
Pole Univ. Leonard de Vinci, Paris La Defense
Preliminary data given
Total energy detection method
Description of C6D6 detector
CERN proton synchrotron
20 GeV/c proton (0.4 pulses/sec, 6 ns width)
on Pb
Two C6D6 liquid scintillators (127.3 mm diam.,
78 mm in length, active volume ~1000 cm3) located at
9.2 cm upstream from the sample centre and
perpendicular to the beam direction.
SiMon detector for flux measurement during runs
Total energy detection with pulse height weighting
technique (PHWT). Sn=7.33 MeV (25Mg), 11.09 MeV (26Mg)
and 6.44 MeV (27Mg) adopted.
Flight path of 185.07+/-0.01 m
Absolute normalization to the Au 4.9 eV resonance
Corrections for
- background in keV region due to in-beam gamma-rays
scattered from sample,
- background due to sample-scattered neutrons,
- neutron multiple scattering,
- self-shielding,
- Doppler broadening,
- experimental resolution
were done during resonance shape analysis by SUMMY.

Analyzed together with transmission data from
ORELA by SAMMY up to 700 keV.
The resonance energy and the partial widths were
allowed to vary while spin and angular momentum were
kept fixed.
Negative resonance parameters were adjusted to
reproduce the thermal cross section by T.A.Walkiewicz
et al.(1992) with the energy from P.M.Endt (1998).
Nuclear radii were adjusted to 5.4 fm (s-wave) and
3.8 fm (p-wave).

with caution

All capture widths may be underestimated by as much

Analyzed together with transmission data from
ORELA by SAMMY up to 700 keV.
The resonance energy and the partial widths were
allowed to vary while spin and angular momentum were
kept fixed.
Negative resonance parameters were adjusted to
reproduce the thermal cross section by T.A.Walkiewicz
et al.(1992) with the energy from P.M.Endt (1998).
Nuclear radii were adjusted to 3.73 fm (both s-and p-
waves).

On. Recompiled.
Resonances contribution in MACS
Direct radiative capture component, DRC
MACS.
The cross sections determined using the
resonance parameters have been convoluted with a
Maxwellian neutron energy distribution to obtain the
actual stellar cross section (MACS).
DRC was considered as a background and was subtracted
from the capture yield and from the resonance cross
section as well.
Method based on Lane-Lynn theory was used to DRC
calculation. Described in REL-REF of A.Mengoni+.
To obtain the actual MACS, the calculated DRC cross
section was added to those extracted by the resonance
shape analysis.
Data are compared with the values in the KADoNiS
database [http://www.kadonis.org].
See ANALYSIS.
Total uncertainty.
To propagate the uncertainties from resonances
parameters to the stellar cross section, a parametric
procedure was adopted. In this procedure, the MACS were
calculated using resonance parameters randomly varied
within uncertainties.
Table V of J,PR/C,85,044615,2012
Res.parameters.
